content.full_text:
Crrv or MoUNTATN VrEw
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
Telephone 415.903.6303
Fax 415.967.421,5
May 77 7997
VIA FAX AND SURFACE MAIL
500 Castro Street
P. O. Box 7540
Mountain View, CA 94039-7540
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Re: Request for Formal Advice letter--Potential Conflict of Interest
Dear Mr. Hallibrin:
This is a request for a formal advice letter.on the following Conllict of Interest
questions. We request your response as soon as possible, as the matter is currently
scheduled to be considered by the City Council on ]une 77,7997.
Councilmember Dena Bonnell owns real property, her personal residence, at 418
Mercy street, in the City of Mountain View. For purposes of this inquiry, you may
assume that her equity is over $100,000. The Residence is located in about the center
of the area of the Old Mountain View Neighborhood Preservation and
Improvement Plan (Study), a consultant study which includes the historic and
geographic center of the City. The location of the Councilmember Bonnell's
residence is marked on the attached map, which also shows the areas in which
neighborhood improvements are recommended.
Mountain View's population is currently about 62500. The residential population
of the Study area is about 4000 residents, or about 5.97o of the total population. The
downtown business area bisects the area, but is not the subject of the plan. The
current draft of the Study recommends a number of general improvements to the
entire area. In some large sub-areas, specific special programs are proposed, such as a
street tree program, Streetscape plans, architectural review and guidelines for
remodels and additions (this including the Councilmember's neighborhood) and, a
comprehensive code enforcement program. A park site designation is also proposed
directly across the street from the Councilmember's residence. A copy of the
Consultant's latest draft of the Study, dated February 7997, is enclosed with the
mailed copy of this letter.
Recycled Paper


Mr. Scott Hallibrin
May 17,1991.
Page2
The Planning Commission has reviewed the Study, and has agreed with the staff
recommendation to the city Council that the majority of the elements of the study
be "considered" when addressing land use proposals in the study area, and that
development proponents who propose to deviate significantly from the study's
recommendations justify those deviations. The recommendation is further that the
architectural design guideline portion of the study not be acted upon, but that the
whole issue be deferred to another city wide study that is currently underway.
Specific and binding implementation of the programs proposed would not result
from the recommended action on the study itseli but would require separate action
of the City Council to allocate funds, adopt necessary ordinances and take other
specific implementing action.
Councilmember Bonnell acknowledges a potential conflict of interest in acting in
any way on the issue of the park site, since it is clear that there is a reasonable
possibility that a determination to approve or reject that site designation would
have a significant impact on the value of her property. She intends to request the
City Council to segregate this issue from the remainder of the discussion and discuss
and act upon it separately from the remainder of the Study, without her
participation.
It is less clear, however, whether Councilmember Bonnell may participate in the
consideration of the rernainder of the Study even if if the park issue is treated
separately. With regard to the area-wide proposals of the Study, it appears that the
plan may affect a significant portion of the public generally (the population of the
Study area) the same way as the Councilmember. With regard to the nearest areas
proposed for special treatment under the neighborhood improvement plan (the
Comprehensive Street Tree Program and street-scape areas, over 300 but less than
2500 feet from the residence) the question is less clear. Both of these areas appear to
affect about 25qo of the entire Study area. Some specific traffic controls are also
within 300 feet. We have no specific information as to whether implementation of
the plan would have any specific impact on the value of the subject property, but if
the entire p]11 werg to be implemented, it can be expected that area-wid-e pioperty
values would be affected.
The specific questions are these:
1. If the issue of the park site designation is segregated, discussed and acted
upon by the remainder of the City Council withouicoiuncilmember Bonnell's
participation, may she participate in consideration of the remainder of the study?


Mr. Scott Hallibrin
May 17,'1991
Page 3
2. In order to participate in the remainder of the study must the proposals for
the nearby areas addressed in the traffic neighborhood improvement segments also
be segregated, and the Councilmember disqualify herself from that discussion?
3. Does Guidelines Section f8702.3(1)(a) and (b) apply to this situation, when
the property in question is located within an overall plan area, but where the sub-
elements potentially likely to cause a conflict are a distance away, but within the
distances specified in that section? It appears that the standards set out in this
section for "indirect" involvement are in many resPects more rigorous ("...unless
the decision will have no financial effect..."--emPhasis added) than those set out in
the "direct" involvement regulation, Section 78702.1.(a)(3), which makes no
mention of the adoption of plans, i.e. General or Specific plans, which in some
circumstances could have substantial effects on value, even if the implementing
zoning or other implementation is not then before the decision maker. The
guideline sections are very confusing in sorting out these elements.
4. Are the answers to any of the questions above different because the real
property in question is the Councilmember's Personally occupied residence, rather
than income-producing property?
Because of the uncertain nature of the answers to the above questions, we are
requesting a formal advice letter.
If you have any questions or need further information,, please do not hesitate to call
encl.

the entire p]11 werg to be implemented, it can be expected that area-wid-e pioperty

upon by the remainder of the City Council withouicoiuncilmember Bonnell's

Mr. Scott Hallibrin
May 17,'1991
2. In order to participate in the remainder of the study must the proposals for

mention of the adoption of plans, i.e. General or Specific plans, which in some
circumstances could have substantial effects on value, even if the implementing
zoning or other implementation is not then before the decision maker. The
guideline sections are very confusing in sorting out these elements.
4. Are the answers to any of the questions above different because the real
property in question is the Councilmember's Personally occupied residence, rather

Because of the uncertain nature of the answers to the above questions, we are
